title: Fines for speeding in Syracuse school zones start next week, but not at every site
summary: Syracuse school zone speed and red-light camera enforcement begins November 3, 2025, after a two-month grace period with warning tickets. Of 28 camera locations installed, 15 are active for enforcement while 10 will be relocated and 3 require school zone boundary adjustments. The program flagged over 95,300 violations in September alone, though the violation rate declined as warning letters were distributed, suggesting behavioral impact.

political_signal.description: City Council members raised procedural concerns about camera placement, visibility, and implementation process, proposing a moratorium. Mayor's administration pledged adjustment plan and grace periods for relocated sites, earning councilors' conditional support. Reflects tension between safety initiative goals and constituent-driven accountability on implementation.

processing_notes: Article date appears to be October 28, 2025 (future date—verify source publication date)
Enforcement start date: November 3, 2025 (one day after stated grace period end of November 2)
